content.claim: Hungary possesses national-level assessment infrastructure (National ABC in grades 6, 8, 10; PISA participation) with public result reporting and school-level requirement to analyze outcomes by student distribution and family background, but PISA data findings have not historically translated into structural policy corrections.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: PISA reception analysis attributes limited impact to 'local cultural contexts and disorganized institutional structures,' which is descriptive rather than mechanistic explanation
'Missing feedback-to-action link' is inferred from observed pattern, not directly documented through implementation case study
Assessment infrastructure existence does not establish that data quality or timeliness is sufficient for rapid policy feedback
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
